title: How to install buildah latest version based on centos7
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

https://github.com/containers/buildah/tree/main/contrib/buildahimage/centos7, kernel version of 3.10.0 needs use centos7 as base image to install buildah, but the latest buildah version of centos7 is 1.11.6(old), but currently the latest version of buildah is 1.22, how can i install buildah v1.22 base on centos7 image ?  

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1.  pull centos:7 image and run it
2.  yum install -y buildah
3.


**Describe the results you received:**

comments.body: This is not an upstream issue. Centos:7 is very old, and does not have updated buildah builds.  You could use podman or docker to run newer versions of buildah in a --privileged container.  Or you could attempt to build from github.
@rhatdan But if my host with old kernel 3.10.0,  the newer version buildah in a --privileged container also can not run. So i need upgrade my host kernel version?
Well I would say you need to move to Centos 8 or RHEL 8.
